item.title: Chevron sees ‘many decades of opportunity’ in Venezuela
item.description: Fiscal framework has improved to an extent but ‘ambiguity’ in law still exists, says Mike Wirth

item.title: US energy chief says up to 3 million bpd expected from Strategic Petroleum Reserve
item.description: DOE Secretary Chris Wright also touted the new US-Venezuela relationship at the conference

item.title: Trump's domestic and foreign policy contrasts create headache for oil majors
item.description: US president's clarity on domestic energy policy contrasts sharply with foreign policy ambitions

item.title: Technip Energies buys stake in green hydrogen-based project
item.description: DEZiR project expected to start operations in 2030 and produce 80,000 tonnes per annum of e-SAF

item.title: European offshore wind investments grew fivefold in one year: Report
item.description: European wind investments will overtake solar this year if current trends keep up, according to a new report

item.title: UK to install new radars that can live with offshore wind
item.description: Industry group says order could clear path for 10GW of projects

item.title: Prio starts Wahoo pre-salt production with higher-than-expected flow rates
item.description: Company expects to achieve output of 40,000 bpd by the end of April

item.title: Russia's largest oil terminal ablaze after drone attack
item.description: Dozens of drones hit storage tanks serving Primorsk oil export terminal

item.title: Oil falls sharply as Trump postpones strikes on Iran energy facilities after 'productive conversations' to end hostilities
item.description: Crude prices fell sharply on Monday after US President Donald Trump announced that he was postponing previously threatened strikes on Iranian energy installations

item.title: Wood appoints new head of consulting
item.description: David Cole joins from UK nuclear power plant project Sizewell C

item.title: Oil rises on mounting threats to energy facilities after fresh Iran warning
item.description: Several Middle East oil and gas facilities have been impacted as part of Iran's retaliation last week to Israel's attack on its South Pars field

item.title: Aramco chief Nasser to pull out of CeraWeek as Middle East conflict escalates
item.description: CERAWeek global 'corporate leaders' conference starts on Monday and is likely to host more than 10,000 participants from over 2350 companies

item.title: Contractors in contest for two large FPSOs in Australia
item.description: World-class contractors are bidding for the long-awaited Browse project's newbuild floaters

item.title: Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Iran's power plants if Hormuz not reopened in 48 hours
item.description: More than 20% of the world's crude oil and LNG usually passes through the Strait of Hormuz, with the shipping route significantly disrupted since the start of the US-Israel attack on Iran

item.title: Oil rises as US set to send thousands of troops to Iran
item.description: Brent settles above $111 while WTI stays below triple digits

item.title: Subsea 7 issues final annual report
item.description: Report boasts of company's strong position leading up to merger with Saipem later this year

item.title: Flemish Pass basin blocks lined up in east Canada bid round
item.description: Newfoundland & Labrador to launch acreage offering in June as it ponders improvements to licensing

item.title: BP and XRG set to start Egypt exploration campaign
item.description: Two-well programme will hunt for additional gas resources near Arcius Energy assets

item.title: US eyes operation to occupy Iran's Kharg Island oil hub: report
item.description: Option being considered in effort to persuade Iran to reopen Strait of Hormuz, according to a US news outlet

item.title: Beijing appoints new CNOOC chief executive
item.description: The new chief executive has served as general manager of CNOOC since 2025

item.title: Lukoil writes off $20 billion of foreign assets in sanctions hit
item.description: Lukoil swings to steep net loss in 2025 as result of write-off, with international subsidiary valued “close to zero”

item.title: BP to quit iconic London office for new global headquarters
item.description: Supermajor will end more than 100 years of history at two sites

item.title: BP to quit iconic London office in move to new headquarters
item.description: Supermajor will end more than 100 years of history at two sites

item.title: BP to move to new London headquarters
item.description: Supermajor to also call time on Sunbury location after presence of more than 100 years

item.title: UK gas terminal strike action extends into sixth month
item.description: Workers at terminal first launched strike action in October last year

item.title: Revamped seismic ready for launch of Equatorial Guinea bid round
item.description: Searcher reprocessing 'transforms' legacy 2D data ahead of 25-block licensing exercise

item.title: Norway's crude production up 15.3% in February
item.description: Country's crude output hit 1.87 million barrels per day in February

item.title: Kazakhstan seeks partner to replace Lukoil in stalled offshore project
item.description: Authorities keen to proceed with project viewed as key for domestic supply chain

item.title: Woodside tops this week's leading oil and gas appointments
Fresh strikes trigger fire, force shut down at major Kuwait oil facility
item.description: The Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery, 50 kilometres south of Kuwait City, has a refining capacity of 346,000 barrels per day
